---
title: "November 26, 2025"
---

## **November 26, 2025** {#november-26,-2025}

### **Present** {#present-3}

* Abdulkareem Alsudais, Armstrong Foundjem,  Ben Hawks, Christine Kirkpatrick, Gary Mazzaferro, Geoffrey Fox, Gregg Barrett, Gregor von Laszewski, Juri Papay, Marco Colombo, Piotr Luszczek, Sebastian Lobentanzer

### **Tentative Agenda** {#tentative-agenda-1}

* Any New Members Introduction  
* White Papers and Published Papers  
  * The Benchmark carpentry white paper [https://www.overleaf.com/project/68f8f5b979760743fe572121](https://www.overleaf.com/project/68f8f5b979760743fe572121)   
  * Paper on Science Benchmarks and Ontologies [http://arxiv.org/abs/2511.05614](http://arxiv.org/abs/2511.05614)   
  * New white papers  
* Any Other Business

### **New Member** {#new-member}

* Sebastian Lobentanzer [https://www.linkedin.com/in/slobentanzer/](https://www.linkedin.com/in/slobentanzer/) is Head of Computational Biology at the German Center for Diabetes Research (Deutsches Zentrum für Diabetesforschung, DZD)

### **Google Meet Notes** {#google-meet-notes-3}

* [MLC Science WG \- 2025/11/26 07:59 PST \- Notes by Gemini](https://docs.google.com/document/d/16RczQwGKC_cfUrJM32TGSAfAfwQ0uqaHEr6c9LG39mI/edit?usp=sharing)   
* The MLC Science WG meeting on November 26, 2025, covered the following key points:  
* New Member: Sebastian Lobentanzer from Helmholz was introduced, expressing interest in automating the scientific process with agentic systems and contributing to carpentries.  
* Carpentry Paper Status: The paper is frozen and awaiting Fermilab's approval. Gary Mazzaferro requested to be acknowledged instead of listed as an author due to a conflict of interest, which was accepted. The paper has been proofread, with a caution against using external AI tools for review.  
* Next Steps for the Paper: Discussions centered on conference submission, with Perk (Minneapolis in July) suggested as a suitable venue.  
* Future Work: The group emphasized developing tutorials or curriculum, possibly in the style of Software Carpentry, to disseminate the paper's findings.  
* Broadening Scope: Gary Mazzaferro proposed a broader framework for assessing AI data center projects, including economic performance, feasibility, and integrating concerns from various stakeholders across the life cycle, which the group found ambitious but timely.  
* Energy Benchmarking: The importance of energy usage and cost in benchmarking was highlighted, with a suggestion for a separate technical paper on this topic, led by Armstrong Foundjem, who needs to first review the existing "ML Perf Power" paper.  
* Action Items: Include sharing the conference link for a workshop in Korea, proofreading the paper by gyuri papay, contacting Fermilab about the approval process, and you sending an email to Sebastian Lobentanzer about giving a talk

### **Discussion** {#discussion-3}

* Gregg Barrett asked Sebastian: I would be interested in the pipeline / toolsets you are using Sebastian. Drop me a note. [gregg.barrett@cirrusai.net](mailto:gregg.barrett@cirrusai.net)  
  * Geoffrey: Sebastian. Could you give a presentation on your use of agentic AI  
* Gregg Barrett noted for Carpentry paper  
  * I have been through it twice Juri. Made some minor edits. It should be near completion. There might be more opportunity to check links and references  
  * gyuri papay; Thank you Gregg.  
* Gregg Barrett with Christine’s thumbs up: I was thinking Carpentry "tools" for benchmarking.  
* Christine Kirkpatrick suggested PEARC for Carpentry paper [https://pearc.acm.org/pearc26/important-dates/](https://pearc.acm.org/pearc26/important-dates/)  
  * Gregg Barrett agreed PEARC would be a good suggestion.  
* Christine noted PEARC workshop:   
* Duration: Full day (6 hours including, 2 brief breaks, approximately 15 minutes each) or half-day (3 hours including 1 approximately 15-minute break).  
* Christine Kirkpatrick There is a datacenter benchmark mentioned here and in their other paper [MLCommons Power Working Group Presents MLPerf Power benchmark at IEEE HPCA Symposium](https://mlcommons.org/2025/03/ml-commons-power-hpca/)   
* [\[2410.12032\] MLPerf Power: Benchmarking the Energy Efficiency of Machine Learning Systems from Microwatts to Megawatts for Sustainable AI](https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.12032)   
* [Benchmark MLPerf Storage | MLCommons V1.1 Results](https://mlcommons.org/benchmarks/storage/)
